summary: Normalize the emit workspace path before deriving harness_root so `.temper` and `./.temper` spell owned paths identically — a live byte-faithful projection is never reaped as ownerless

edit.description: emit() derives `harness_root = workspace_dir.parent()` (drift.rs:634); `./.temper` parents to `.` while `.temper` parents to `` — so `owned_paths` spell `./docs/...` while the prior lock's rows spell `docs/...`, and read_prior_provenance's rows miss the owned set → a live byte-faithful projection is reaped and deleted (drift.rs:847-863, reap_or_report_orphan:1314). Normalize workspace_dir (strip the leading `./` component) before deriving harness_root.

tests.asserts: emitting into `./.temper` after a lock written under `.temper` reaps no live projection and reports it unchanged — never both reaped and unchanged

acceptance: emit into `./.temper` and `.temper` derive identical owned paths; a live projection a differently-spelled lock owns is reported unchanged, never reaped (pipeline.md Emit: reap only a byte-faithful ownerless file).
notes: Dogfood second-cut finding 1 (observed c2f8a2c), reproduced twice in examples/base-harness; root cause of first-cut finding 2 (install --yes --dry-run previews reaped+unchanged for one live projection). Destructive: the emit-hash safety line passes because the file is byte-faithful, so the reap deletes it (drift.rs:1314). scoped at b676656.

summary: Let a composed embedded kind's `render` hook project fence-free — renderMemberFence wraps every blocks() value in a `member.<kind> <key>` fence, against the writer-only, unconstrained embedded format

edit.description: renderMemberFence (emit.ts:139) always wraps a member's body in a ```member.<kind> <key>``` fence even when the kind declares a `render` hook (the comment asserts 'the fence wrapper itself never changes'); the model makes a composed host's embedded format writer-only and unconstrained (no admissibility bar) and the engine never reads the fence back. Let a render-hook kind's output project without the fence; resolveBody (172) joins the blocks.

tests.asserts: a blocks() body of a render-hook kind projects as the hook's markdown with no member.<kind> fence; a render-less kind's projection is byte-unchanged

acceptance: A composed corpus with render-hook embedded kinds projects readable markdown, not stacked code fences; the lock's nested-member facts are unchanged (representation.md kind: an embedded format is writer-only and unconstrained).
notes: Dogfood second-cut finding 2 (observed c2f8a2c), reproduced in examples/base-harness. The fence fold is retired — nested members build from lock rows (src/extract.rs nested_members_from_rows) — so the fence is cosmetic and safe to drop for render-hook kinds; facts ride the lock. scoped at b676656.

summary: Admit embedded members into the SDK mentionable set + export a Member→Mentionable adapter — declaredAddresses is requirements + top-level kind:name only, so an embedded-member mention falsely dangles

edit.description: declaredAddresses (declarations.ts:551) = declaredRequirements + top-level `kind:name`; add each embedded member's host-scoped address (the lock's `<member>/<kind>/<key>` leaf grammar src/read.rs parse_leaf_address already resolves) so a mention to an embedded member's leaf resolves rather than dangles. contract.md 'edge': a mention's target may be a member or a leaf.

export a Member→Mentionable adapter — the example hand-rolls `mentionOf` (examples/base-harness/.temper/kinds.ts) to spell a member's `kind:name` mention target; capture the two-line convention once beside the Mentionable interface (prose.ts:16).

tests.asserts: a mention targeting an embedded member's host-scoped leaf address resolves without a dangling-mention refusal; the Member→Mentionable adapter spells kind:name

acceptance: A decision can cite an embedded member's invariant as a graph edge without a false dangling-mention refusal; the address is host-scoped (system:scanner/invariant/done-is-exact), never a flat invariant:<key> (contract.md edge: a mention's target may be a member or a leaf).
notes: Dogfood second-cut findings 4+5 (observed c2f8a2c). Position: host-scoped addresses (the lock's existing leaf grammar), never flat `invariant:<key>` — flat would force corpus-wide key uniqueness on embedded kinds. A different grammar is a fork; surfaced. The adapter is finding 4's convention capture. Verify the builtin_lock_frozen seam if mention rows shift. scoped at b676656.

summary: Defer the SDK's required-requirement fill check to the engine — checkFills refuses at emit(program) on a required requirement filled only by a layout document's satisfies slot it cannot see

edit.description: checkFills (emit.ts:196) builds `filled` from composed members' satisfies only; a `required` requirement whose fills live in a layout document's edge slot refuses ('unfilled required requirement', emit.ts:221) before the engine reads the document and derives its satisfies rows (src/drift.rs merges layout satisfies at emit, 672). The SDK implements no semantics — defer this fill enforcement to the engine's emit-time requirement clause, which sees composed + layout-derived satisfies. The dangling-satisfies check (200) stays. Serialized behind EMBED-RENDER-FENCE-FREE (shared file).

tests.asserts: a required requirement with no composed satisfier does not refuse at the SDK emit phase when a layout-content kind could fill it; a genuinely-dangling satisfies still refuses

acceptance: A layout-fill corpus emits without a spurious SDK-phase unfilled-required refusal; the engine's requirement clause remains the fill gate over composed + layout satisfies (pipeline.md: the SDK implements no semantics).
notes: Dogfood first-cut finding 1 (observed 4e111af); gap stands for any layout-fill corpus (the example moved fills program-side to dodge it). Position: fill enforcement is the engine's requirement-cardinality clause, not an SDK pre-flight; the alternative (the SDK reads the layout too) re-implements engine semantics — rejected. scoped at b676656.

summary: The packaging remainder after the linux-x64+win32-x64 first cut: darwin binaries (Apple notarizing), the plugin/bundle + marketplace.json, and the binary↔npm version lockstep at the v0.1 tag

gate.reason: Human release actions: John's Apple notarizing for the darwin standalone binary + darwin optionalDependency package; the v0.1 launch tag that flips the binary's self-reported crate version (0.1.0) into lockstep with npm's 0.0.x. Channel 3 (plugin/bundle + marketplace.json) is a git repo + marketplace.json + free community web form (verified 2026-07-06, code.claude.com/docs) — no signing creds — but rides the same release-workflow build-out. release.yml (channel 2, linux-x64+win32-x64) + the SDK launcher/optionalDependencies + standalone tag assets shipped 07-11; the standalone-asset step is wired but unexercised until a tag fires.

edit.description: Extend the existing release.yml (linux-x64 + win32-x64 first cut) with the darwin matrix targets (aarch64/x86_64-apple-darwin) + an Apple notarization step, and a channel-3 step producing/publishing the `temper bundle` output with its marketplace.json. Distinct from the standalone tag-asset step, already wired (unexercised until a tag).

acceptance: A stranger acquires temper on all three OSes without a Rust toolchain (darwin binary notarized + pinned), or via the plugin/bundle (marketplace.json); the binary self-reports 0.1.0 in lockstep with the v0.1 npm cut (distribution.md: prebuilt binaries install on all three OSes; lockstep engine pin).
notes: Supersedes retired PACKAGING-CHANNELS (John, 07-11): both its file claims were dead — release.yml now exists (its `new` claim fails the refs gate) and the root-package.json launcher was ruled out (the launcher + optionalDependencies live on the SDK, distribution.md 'What ships'). Release infra outside src/; cargo gates can't validate npm/bundle publish. scoped at b676656.
